rfc_references.title: The Base16, Base32, and Base64 Data Encodings

derived_from: RFC 4648 — The Base16, Base32, and Base64 Data Encodings, October 2006
RFC 4648 Section 10 — Test vectors for Base64, Base32, Base16
Test vectors verified by running Python 3.14.3 base64 module on macOS/Darwin 25.3.0, 2026-03-30
notes: All encode/decode test vectors are from RFC 4648 Section 10, which specifies the canonical test suite for base64.
The URL-safe alphabet (RFC 4648 §5) replaces '+' with '-' and '/' with '_'. Python's urlsafe_b64encode/urlsafe_b64decode implement this alphabet.
b64encode() and b64decode() work with bytes objects. b64decode() also accepts str input.

Note on spec storage: args of type bytes_b64 store the base64 encoding of the raw input bytes. For RFC 4648 §10 inputs (b'', b'f', ..., b'foobar'), the bytes_b64 storage value is identical to the b64encode output because these inputs happen to encode to the same base64 alphabet. The description field clarifies the test intent.

library.notes: base64 is a Python stdlib module. It delegates to binascii for the actual encoding/decoding.

alphabet_sizes.name: BASE64_ALPHABET_SIZE
alphabet_sizes.description: Standard base64 uses 64 printable characters (A-Z, a-z, 0-9, +, /)

alphabet_sizes.name: BASE64_PAD_CHAR
alphabet_sizes.description: ASCII code of the padding character '='

encoded_bytes.description: Output of b64encode(): a bytes object containing only the characters A-Z, a-z, 0-9, +, /, and = (padding). Length is always a multiple of 4.

base64.b64encode.description: Encode bytes using the standard Base64 alphabet (RFC 4648 §4). Output is a bytes object whose length is a multiple of 4, padded with '=' as needed.

parameters.name: altchars
parameters.description: Optional 2-byte string to replace '+' and '/'. Default None uses standard alphabet.

postconditions: all(c in b'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789+/=' for c in b64encode(s))

base64.b64decode.description: Decode Base64-encoded bytes or ASCII string. Raises binascii.Error on invalid input when validate=True.

parameters.name: validate
parameters.description: If True, only base64 alphabet characters are allowed. Default False.

return_values.binascii.Error: If s contains characters outside the base64 alphabet and validate=True

base64.urlsafe_b64encode.description: Encode bytes using the URL-safe Base64 alphabet (RFC 4648 §5): uses '-' instead of '+' and '_' instead of '/'.

base64.urlsafe_b64decode.description: Decode URL-safe Base64-encoded bytes or ASCII string.

base64.b32encode.description: Encode bytes using the Base32 alphabet (RFC 4648 §6): A-Z and 2-7.

base64.b16encode.description: Encode bytes using the Base16 (hex) alphabet (RFC 4648 §8): 0-9 and A-F (uppercase).

invariants.description: b64encode(b'') == b'' — RFC 4648 §10 test vector 1
b64encode(b'f') == b'Zg==' — RFC 4648 §10 test vector 2
b64encode(b'fo') == b'Zm8=' — RFC 4648 §10 test vector 3
b64encode(b'foo') == b'Zm9v' — RFC 4648 §10 test vector 4 (no padding needed)
b64encode(b'foob') == b'Zm9vYg==' — RFC 4648 §10 test vector 5
b64encode(b'fooba') == b'Zm9vYmE=' — RFC 4648 §10 test vector 6
b64encode(b'foobar') == b'Zm9vYmFy' — RFC 4648 §10 test vector 7 (no padding needed)
b64decode(b'Zg==') == b'f' — inverse of encode vector 2
b64decode(b'Zm9v') == b'foo' — inverse of encode vector 4
b64decode(b'Zm9vYmFy') == b'foobar' — inverse of encode vector 7
b64decode decodes a known binary sequence (0xFB 0xFF 0xFE) from standard alphabet
urlsafe_b64encode replaces '+' with '-' and '/' with '_' for data that produces those chars in standard base64
urlsafe_b64decode decodes the URL-safe alphabet back to the original bytes
b32encode(b'foo') — RFC 4648 §10 base32 test vector
b32encode(b'foobar') — RFC 4648 §10 base32 test vector
b16encode(b'foo') == b'666F6F' — RFC 4648 §10 base16 test vector (uppercase hex)
b16encode of binary bytes produces uppercase hex: b'\xde\xad\xbe\xef' -> b'DEADBEEF'
b64encode then b64decode is lossless for arbitrary binary data
urlsafe_b64encode then urlsafe_b64decode is lossless for arbitrary binary data including bytes that produce +/
b64decode with validate=True raises binascii.Error on non-base64 characters

base64_encoding.description: Base64 encodes every 3 bytes of input as 4 ASCII characters from the 64-character alphabet. The output length is always ceil(n/3)*4. If n is not divisible by 3, one or two '=' padding characters are appended.

fields.name: encoded_groups
fields.description: Sequence of 4-character groups, each encoding 3 bytes of input. Last group may contain 1 or 2 '=' padding characters.

error_model.return_code_semantics: base64 uses Python exceptions rather than integer return codes.
error_model.error_stickiness: No stateful error model — all functions are stateless.

error_codes.name: binascii.Error
error_codes.meaning: Invalid base64 data. Raised by b64decode(s, validate=True) if s contains characters outside the base64 alphabet.
